mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of periods during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where retail sales drop exceeds the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total payroll employment and a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market slack and weakening productivity, which suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no statistical edge over single-series baselines, or 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of periods when industrial production declines during CPIAUCSL readings above 290.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while inflation remains elevated, the probability of a hard landing increases, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02, or 60-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of high-inflation tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong residential investment demand, which boosts construction sector earnings and provides a positive tailwind for S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or 20-day returns are negative in greater than 55% of periods with rising housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0% triggers recession fears, prompting rapid portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP confirms an economic downturn, which systematically reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during disinflationary phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of quarters following a negative GDPC1 reading, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
